item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good, solid concept executed thoroughly. Manages to avoid both the classic 'this is just the folklore, SCPified' pitfall of mythological articles, and the fatigue that often comes when an article has loads of iterative descriptions. The fact that this elegantly squares the vagueness and lack of coherency in descriptions of the Bunyip. Nice job. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, I really love this. It's a simple concept executed beautifully, with a very believable progression in both the Foundation's understanding of it and their response. I enjoy biology-based SCPs, and this one is done really well. The ethics question at the end - can they wipe out over half of the world's marsupials to enact effective containment - is a compelling one.

The effect itself, of making you see a big scary monster, is kind of simple/bland, but I think it works because that's not really the focus of the SCP, and isn't really relevant to the core message.

Great article, thoroughly enjoyed, easy +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the feedback. Yes, I appreciate the length is going to be an issue for a lot of people. All I can really say is that I did not set out for it to be this long but it kind of got away from me.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Fixed (hopefully) with new image. Sorry about that. I knew what NC stood for but mistakenly believed as long as I wasn't using it commercially it was acceptable. Guess I should have read the guide more carefully.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like this Scp, but its length makes it tedious, I appreciate that you recognized this and made differences in the words blue but in the end it makes reading a confusing mess, because most of the important words are to most of the public jargon, this makes reading the first one a chore because you have to either google or try and remember what some of the words mean. This makes highlighting the blue words ineffective because by the time you on to reading the new page you have forgotten the last one and the whole thing loses its context. May I just recommend reducing the size because half of the pages, while I can appreciate the work you put into them, do not add anything to this Scp.

Regardless it is a good concept and you definitely proved yourself as a good writer with a large vocab and technical skills especially considering it is your first Scp. Just have a look at some of the more popular Scp's though, almost all of them do not have half as many pages as this one, because unfortunately and I wish it wasn't the case but no everyone can be bothered to read through so many pages that uses jargon they do not understand so consistently. Definitely a +1 though, not often you find a Scp with the main problem being too long problem is being to long.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Concurring with Ihp, the second image has been taken down for now due to it being non-compliant. However, if you can find an image that is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0), then please do source it and replace it with the image source for the current second image.

EDIT: The image has been replaced with a cc-compliant alternative.

item.title: Re: Thanks for Reading
item.content:encoded:
Not staff, but I'm pretty sure you can't use CC-BY-NC images on the wiki- "NC" is non-commercial, and the wiki uses a commercial license.

That said, I like this. Will elaborate more later, but first now, +1.

item.title: Thanks for Reading
item.content:encoded:
First SCP. Thanks for reading.

Special thanks go to RockTeethMothEyes, Crashington and tupacofficial for their invaluable assistance through the concept and drafting process.

+ Image Sources
- Hide

Charles Douglas Richardson (1853-1932) [Public domain]
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bunyip_by_C.D._Richardson.jpg
Victor Prout? [Public domain]
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bagged_thylacine.jpg
"Glass Negative - Wombat, by A.J. Campbell, Flinders Island, Bass Strait, Tasmania, Oct 1909" by A.J. Campbell is licensed under CC PDM 1.0
https://search.creativecommons.org/photos/b17f1854-01f2-4359-8f3c-05604ebacd40
"Kangaroo at Australia Zoo-02+" by Sheba_Also 43,000 photos is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0
https://search.creativecommons.org/photos/b6848189-0831-45e7-a3d2-66beec8cc506
"Whole lotta koalas!" by TheGirlsNY is licensed under CC BY-SA 2.0
https://search.creativecommons.org/photos/c00f5819-ec5d-400b-a32f-7efe2a5a5fc0

For anyone interested in the real-life circumstances inspiring some of the addenda, I’ve included some Wikipedia links below on those issues (definitely not required reading).

+ Links
- Hide

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devil_facial_tumour_disease
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Telefomin_cuscus
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Quoll#Threats
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Numbat#Conservation_status
